frameworks.name: multi-task reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: learning transferable policies across tasks with changing reward functions

frameworks.name: successor features
frameworks.one_line: maps prior policies and encountered features to new reward functions

frameworks.name: universal value function approximators
frameworks.one_line: approximates value functions across multiple tasks

core_question: How do humans transfer knowledge across reinforcement-learning tasks with changing reward functions?
key_assumptions: tasks share features but differ in reward function
human learning can be characterized by candidate multi-task RL algorithms
two-step decision making captures policy reuse
key_findings: human behavior best matches strategy mapping prior policies to novel scenarios
this account outperforms value-function approximation across tasks
it also outperforms standard model-based and model-free baselines
result replicates in a large preregistered experiment
interpretations: humans generalize across tasks by reusing learned policies under new reward demands
enriches understanding of human RL in complex, changing environments
